commitId.description: The ID of the last commit made to the branch.

approvalRuleTemplateNames.description: The names of all the approval rule templates found in the Amazon Web Services Region for your Amazon Web Services account.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the next results of the operation.

repositories.description: Lists the repositories called by the list repositories operation.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the results of the operation. Batch sizes are 1,000 for list repository operations. When the client sends the token back to CodeCommit, another page of 1,000 records is retrieved.

associatedRepositoryNames.description: A list of names of the repositories that have been associated with the template.

errors.description: A list of any errors that might have occurred while attempting to create the association between the template and the repositories.

conflicts.description: A list of conflicts for each file, including the conflict metadata and the hunks of the differences between the files.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

errors.description: A list of any errors returned while describing the merge conflicts for each file.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the destination commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID of the source commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

baseCommitId.description: The commit ID of the merge base.

conflictMetadata.description: Metadata about a conflict in a merge operation.

filePath.description: The path of the file that contains conflicts.

fileSizes.description: The file sizes of the file in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The size of a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The size of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The size of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

fileModes.description: The file modes of the file in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The file mode of a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The file mode of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The file mode of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

objectTypes.description: Information about any object type conflicts in a merge operation.

source.description: The type of the object in the source branch.

destination.description: The type of the object in the destination branch.

base.description: The type of the object in the base commit of the merge.

numberOfConflicts.description: The number of conflicts, including both hunk conflicts and metadata conflicts.

isBinaryFile.description: A boolean value (true or false) indicating whether the file is binary or textual in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The binary or non-binary status of file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The binary or non-binary status of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The binary or non-binary status of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

contentConflict.description: A boolean value indicating whether there are conflicts in the content of a file.

fileModeConflict.description: A boolean value indicating whether there are conflicts in the file mode of a file.

objectTypeConflict.description: A boolean value (true or false) indicating whether there are conflicts between the branches in the object type of a file, folder, or submodule.

mergeOperations.description: Whether an add, modify, or delete operation caused the conflict between the source and destination of the merge.

source.description: The operation (add, modify, or delete) on a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The operation on a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

mergeHunks.description: A list of hunks that contain the differences between files or lines causing the conflict.

disassociatedRepositoryNames.description: A list of repository names that have had their association with the template removed.

errors.description: A list of any errors that might have occurred while attempting to remove the association between the template and the repositories.

commits.description: An array of commit data type objects, each of which contains information about a specified commit.

errors.description: Returns any commit IDs for which information could not be found. For example, if one of the commit IDs was a shortened SHA ID or that commit was not found in the specified repository, the ID returns an error object with more information.

commitId.description: The full SHA ID of the specified commit.

parents.description: A list of parent commits for the specified commit. Each parent commit ID is the full commit ID.

message.description: The commit message associated with the specified commit.

author.description: Information about the author of the specified commit. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the author, and the email address for the author, as configured in Git.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

committer.description:
Information about the person who committed the specified commit, also known as the committer. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the committer, and the email address for the committer, as configured in Git.

For more information about the difference between an author and a committer in Git, see Viewing the Commit History in Pro Git by Scott Chacon and Ben Straub.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

additionalData.description: Any other data associated with the specified commit.

repositories.description: A list of repositories returned by the batch get repositories operation.

repositoriesNotFound.description: Returns a list of repository names for which information could not be found.

errors.description: Returns information about any errors returned when attempting to retrieve information about the repositories.

accountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account associated with the repository.

repositoryDescription.description: A comment or description about the repository.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the repository was last modified, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the repository was created, in timestamp format.

cloneUrlHttp.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over HTTPS.

cloneUrlSsh.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over SSH.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the repository.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service encryption key used to encrypt and decrypt the repository.

approvalRuleTemplate.description: The content and structure of the created approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateId.description: The system-generated ID of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateName.description: The name of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateDescription.description: The description of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateContent.description: The content of the approval rule template.

ruleContentSha256.description: The SHA-256 hash signature for the content of the approval rule template.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date the approval rule template was most recently changed, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date the approval rule template was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedUser.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who made the most recent changes to the approval rule template.

commitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that contains your committed file changes.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains the commited file changes.

filesAdded.description: The files added as part of the committed file changes.

filesUpdated.description: The files updated as part of the commited file changes.

filesDeleted.description: The files deleted as part of the committed file changes.

absolutePath.description: The full path to the file to be added or updated, including the name of the file.

blobId.description: The blob ID that contains the file information.

fileMode.description: The extrapolated file mode permissions for the file. Valid values include EXECUTABLE and NORMAL.

pullRequest.description: Information about the newly created pull request.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

title.description: The user-defined title of the pull request. This title is displayed in the list of pull requests to other repository users.

description.description: The user-defined description of the pull request. This description can be used to clarify what should be reviewed and other details of the request.

lastActivityDate.description: The day and time of the last user or system activity on the pull request, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the pull request was originally created, in timestamp format.

pullRequestStatus.description: The status of the pull request. Pull request status can only change from OPEN to CLOSED.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who created the pull request.

pullRequestTargets.description: The targets of the pull request, including the source branch and destination branch for the pull request.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

revisionId.description: The system-generated revision ID for the pull request.

approvalRules.description: The approval rules applied to the pull request.

approvalRuleId.description: The system-generated ID of the approval rule.

ruleContentSha256.description: The SHA-256 hash signature for the content of the approval rule.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date the approval rule was most recently changed, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date the approval rule was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedUser.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who made the most recent changes to the approval rule.

originApprovalRuleTemplate.description: The approval rule template used to create the rule.

approvalRuleTemplateId.description: The ID of the template that created the approval rule.

approvalRuleTemplateName.description: The name of the template that created the approval rule.

accountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account associated with the repository.

repositoryDescription.description: A comment or description about the repository.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the repository was last modified, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the repository was created, in timestamp format.

cloneUrlHttp.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over HTTPS.

cloneUrlSsh.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over SSH.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the repository.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service encryption key used to encrypt and decrypt the repository.

commitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that contains your merge results.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains the merge results.

approvalRuleTemplateId.description: The system-generated ID of the deleted approval rule template. If the template has been previously deleted, the only response is a 200 OK.

deletedBranch.description: Information about the branch deleted by the operation, including the branch name and the commit ID that was the tip of the branch.

commitId.description: The ID of the last commit made to the branch.

comment.description: Information about the comment you just deleted.

inReplyTo.description: The ID of the comment for which this comment is a reply, if any.

creationDate.description: The date and time the comment was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the comment was most recently modified, in timestamp format.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the person who posted the comment.

deleted.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the comment has been deleted.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

callerReactions.description: The emoji reactions to a comment, if any, submitted by the user whose credentials are associated with the call to the API.

reactionCounts.description: A string to integer map that represents the number of individual users who have responded to a comment with the specified reactions.

commitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that contains the change that deletes the file.

blobId.description: The blob ID removed from the tree as part of deleting the file.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains the delete file change.

filePath.description: The fully qualified path to the file to be deleted, including the full name and extension of that file.

approvalRuleId.description:
The ID of the deleted approval rule.

If the approval rule was deleted in an earlier API call, the response is 200 OK without content.

repositoryId.description: The ID of the repository that was deleted.

conflictMetadata.description: Contains metadata about the conflicts found in the merge.

mergeHunks.description: A list of merge hunks of the differences between the files or lines.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the destination commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID of the source commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

baseCommitId.description: The commit ID of the merge base.

isConflict.description: A Boolean value indicating whether a combination of hunks contains a conflict. Conflicts occur when the same file or the same lines in a file were modified in both the source and destination of a merge or pull request. Valid values include true, false, and null. True when the hunk represents a conflict and one or more files contains a line conflict. File mode conflicts in a merge do not set this to true.

source.description: Information about the merge hunk in the source of a merge or pull request.

startLine.description: The start position of the hunk in the merge result.

endLine.description: The end position of the hunk in the merge result.

hunkContent.description: The base-64 encoded content of the hunk merged region that might contain a conflict.

destination.description: Information about the merge hunk in the destination of a merge or pull request.

startLine.description: The start position of the hunk in the merge result.

endLine.description: The end position of the hunk in the merge result.

hunkContent.description: The base-64 encoded content of the hunk merged region that might contain a conflict.

base.description: Information about the merge hunk in the base of a merge or pull request.

startLine.description: The start position of the hunk in the merge result.

endLine.description: The end position of the hunk in the merge result.

hunkContent.description: The base-64 encoded content of the hunk merged region that might contain a conflict.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

eventDate.description: The day and time of the pull request event, in timestamp format.

pullRequestEventType.description: The type of the pull request event (for example, a status change event (PULL_REQUEST_STATUS_CHANGED) or update event (PULL_REQUEST_SOURCE_REFERENCE_UPDATED)).

actorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user whose actions resulted in the event. Examples include updating the pull request with more commits or changing the status of a pull request.

pullRequestCreatedEventMetadata.description: Information about the source and destination branches for the pull request.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository where the pull request was created.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID on the source branch used when the pull request was created.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the tip of the branch specified as the destination branch when the pull request was created.

mergeBase.description: The commit ID of the most recent commit that the source branch and the destination branch have in common.

pullRequestStatusChangedEventMetadata.description: Information about the change in status for the pull request event.

pullRequestStatus.description: The changed status of the pull request.

pullRequestSourceReferenceUpdatedEventMe.description: Information about the updated source branch for the pull request event.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository where the pull request was updated.

beforeCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit in the destination branch that was the tip of the branch at the time the pull request was updated.

afterCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit in the source branch that was the tip of the branch at the time the pull request was updated.

mergeBase.description: The commit ID of the most recent commit that the source branch and the destination branch have in common.

pullRequestMergedStateChangedEventMetada.description: Information about the change in mergability state for the pull request event.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository where the pull request was created.

destinationReference.description: The name of the branch that the pull request is merged into.

mergeMetadata.description: Information about the merge state change event.

isMerged.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the merge has been made.

mergedBy.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who merged the branches.

mergeCommitId.description: The commit ID for the merge commit, if any.

mergeOption.description: The merge strategy used in the merge.

approvalRuleId.description: The system-generated ID of the approval rule.

approvalStateChangedEventMetadata.description: Information about an approval state change for a pull request.

revisionId.description: The revision ID of the pull request when the approval state changed.

approvalStatus.description: The approval status for the pull request.

approvalRuleOverriddenEventMetadata.description: Information about an approval rule override event for a pull request.

revisionId.description: The revision ID of the pull request when the override event occurred.

evaluation.description: The result of the evaluation, including the names of the rules whose conditions have been met (if any), the names of the rules whose conditions have not been met (if any), whether the pull request is in the approved state, and whether the pull request approval rule has been set aside by an override.

approved.description: Whether the state of the pull request is approved.

overridden.description: Whether the approval rule requirements for the pull request have been overridden and no longer need to be met.

approvalRulesSatisfied.description: The names of the approval rules that have had their conditions met.

approvalRulesNotSatisfied.description: The names of the approval rules that have not had their conditions met.

approvalRuleTemplate.description: The content and structure of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateId.description: The system-generated ID of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateName.description: The name of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateDescription.description: The description of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateContent.description: The content of the approval rule template.

ruleContentSha256.description: The SHA-256 hash signature for the content of the approval rule template.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date the approval rule template was most recently changed, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date the approval rule template was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedUser.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who made the most recent changes to the approval rule template.

content.description: The content of the blob, usually a file.

inReplyTo.description: The ID of the comment for which this comment is a reply, if any.

creationDate.description: The date and time the comment was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the comment was most recently modified, in timestamp format.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the person who posted the comment.

deleted.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the comment has been deleted.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

callerReactions.description: The emoji reactions to a comment, if any, submitted by the user whose credentials are associated with the call to the API.

reactionCounts.description: A string to integer map that represents the number of individual users who have responded to a comment with the specified reactions.

reactionsForComment.description: An array of reactions to the specified comment.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

emoji.description: The Emoji Version 1.0 graphic of the reaction. These graphics are interpreted slightly differently on different operating systems.

shortCode.description: The emoji short code for the reaction. Short codes are interpreted slightly differently on different operating systems.

reactionUsers.description: The Amazon Resource Names (ARNs) of users who have provided reactions to the comment.

reactionsFromDeletedUsersCount.description: A numerical count of users who reacted with the specified emoji whose identities have been subsequently deleted from IAM. While these IAM users or roles no longer exist, the reactions might still appear in total reaction counts.

commentsForComparedCommitData.description: A list of comment objects on the compared commit.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository that contains the compared commits.

beforeCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit used to establish the before of the comparison.

afterCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit used to establish the after of the comparison.

beforeBlobId.description: The full blob ID of the commit used to establish the before of the comparison.

afterBlobId.description: The full blob ID of the commit used to establish the after of the comparison.

location.description: Location information about the comment on the comparison, including the file name, line number, and whether the version of the file where the comment was made is BEFORE or AFTER.

filePath.description: The name of the file being compared, including its extension and subdirectory, if any.

filePosition.description: The position of a change in a compared file, in line number format.

relativeFileVersion.description: In a comparison of commits or a pull request, whether the change is in the before or after of that comparison.

comments.description: An array of comment objects. Each comment object contains information about a comment on the comparison between commits.

commentsForPullRequestData.description: An array of comment objects on the pull request.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository that contains the pull request.

beforeCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that was the tip of the destination branch when the pull request was created. This commit is superceded by the after commit in the source branch when and if you merge the source branch into the destination branch.

afterCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that was the tip of the source branch at the time the comment was made.

beforeBlobId.description: The full blob ID of the file on which you want to comment on the destination commit.

afterBlobId.description: The full blob ID of the file on which you want to comment on the source commit.

location.description: Location information about the comment on the pull request, including the file name, line number, and whether the version of the file where the comment was made is BEFORE (destination branch) or AFTER (source branch).

filePath.description: The name of the file being compared, including its extension and subdirectory, if any.

filePosition.description: The position of a change in a compared file, in line number format.

relativeFileVersion.description: In a comparison of commits or a pull request, whether the change is in the before or after of that comparison.

comments.description: An array of comment objects. Each comment object contains information about a comment on the pull request.

commit.description: A commit data type object that contains information about the specified commit.

commitId.description: The full SHA ID of the specified commit.

parents.description: A list of parent commits for the specified commit. Each parent commit ID is the full commit ID.

message.description: The commit message associated with the specified commit.

author.description: Information about the author of the specified commit. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the author, and the email address for the author, as configured in Git.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

committer.description:
Information about the person who committed the specified commit, also known as the committer. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the committer, and the email address for the committer, as configured in Git.

For more information about the difference between an author and a committer in Git, see Viewing the Commit History in Pro Git by Scott Chacon and Ben Straub.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

additionalData.description: Any other data associated with the specified commit.

differences.description: A data type object that contains information about the differences, including whether the difference is added, modified, or deleted (A, D, M).

NextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

beforeBlob.description: Information about a beforeBlob data type object, including the ID, the file mode permission code, and the path.

path.description: The path to the blob and associated file name, if any.

mode.description:
The file mode permissions of the blob. File mode permission codes include:

  • 100644 indicates read/write

  • 100755 indicates read/write/execute

  • 160000 indicates a submodule

  • 120000 indicates a symlink

afterBlob.description: Information about an afterBlob data type object, including the ID, the file mode permission code, and the path.

path.description: The path to the blob and associated file name, if any.

mode.description:
The file mode permissions of the blob. File mode permission codes include:

  • 100644 indicates read/write

  • 100755 indicates read/write/execute

  • 160000 indicates a submodule

  • 120000 indicates a symlink

changeType.description: Whether the change type of the difference is an addition (A), deletion (D), or modification (M).

commitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit that contains the content returned by GetFile.

blobId.description: The blob ID of the object that represents the file content.

filePath.description: The fully qualified path to the specified file. Returns the name and extension of the file.

fileMode.description:
The extrapolated file mode permissions of the blob. Valid values include strings such as EXECUTABLE and not numeric values.

The file mode permissions returned by this API are not the standard file mode permission values, such as 100644, but rather extrapolated values. See the supported return values.

fileSize.description: The size of the contents of the file, in bytes.

fileContent.description: The base-64 encoded binary data object that represents the content of the file.

commitId.description: The full commit ID used as a reference for the returned version of the folder content.

folderPath.description: The fully qualified path of the folder whose contents are returned.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains the folder.

subFolders.description: The list of folders that exist under the specified folder, if any.

files.description: The list of files in the specified folder, if any.

symbolicLinks.description: The list of symbolic links to other files and folders in the specified folder, if any.

subModules.description: The list of submodules in the specified folder, if any.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains the folder.

absolutePath.description: The fully qualified path of the folder in the repository.

relativePath.description: The relative path of the specified folder from the folder where the query originated.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID of the source commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the destination commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

baseCommitId.description: The commit ID of the merge base.

mergedCommitId.description: The commit ID for the merge commit created when the source branch was merged into the destination branch. If the fast-forward merge strategy was used, there is no merge commit.

mergeable.description: A Boolean value that indicates whether the code is mergeable by the specified merge option.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the destination commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID of the source commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

baseCommitId.description: The commit ID of the merge base.

conflictMetadataList.description: A list of metadata for any conflicting files. If the specified merge strategy is FAST_FORWARD_MERGE, this list is always empty.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used in a request to return the next batch of the results.

filePath.description: The path of the file that contains conflicts.

fileSizes.description: The file sizes of the file in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The size of a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The size of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The size of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

fileModes.description: The file modes of the file in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The file mode of a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The file mode of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The file mode of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

objectTypes.description: Information about any object type conflicts in a merge operation.

source.description: The type of the object in the source branch.

destination.description: The type of the object in the destination branch.

base.description: The type of the object in the base commit of the merge.

numberOfConflicts.description: The number of conflicts, including both hunk conflicts and metadata conflicts.

isBinaryFile.description: A boolean value (true or false) indicating whether the file is binary or textual in the source, destination, and base of the merge.

source.description: The binary or non-binary status of file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The binary or non-binary status of a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

base.description: The binary or non-binary status of a file in the base of a merge or pull request.

contentConflict.description: A boolean value indicating whether there are conflicts in the content of a file.

fileModeConflict.description: A boolean value indicating whether there are conflicts in the file mode of a file.

objectTypeConflict.description: A boolean value (true or false) indicating whether there are conflicts between the branches in the object type of a file, folder, or submodule.

mergeOperations.description: Whether an add, modify, or delete operation caused the conflict between the source and destination of the merge.

source.description: The operation (add, modify, or delete) on a file in the source of a merge or pull request.

destination.description: The operation on a file in the destination of a merge or pull request.

mergeOptions.description: The merge option or strategy used to merge the code.

sourceCommitId.description: The commit ID of the source commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

destinationCommitId.description: The commit ID of the destination commit specifier that was used in the merge evaluation.

baseCommitId.description: The commit ID of the merge base.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

title.description: The user-defined title of the pull request. This title is displayed in the list of pull requests to other repository users.

description.description: The user-defined description of the pull request. This description can be used to clarify what should be reviewed and other details of the request.

lastActivityDate.description: The day and time of the last user or system activity on the pull request, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the pull request was originally created, in timestamp format.

pullRequestStatus.description: The status of the pull request. Pull request status can only change from OPEN to CLOSED.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who created the pull request.

pullRequestTargets.description: The targets of the pull request, including the source branch and destination branch for the pull request.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

revisionId.description: The system-generated revision ID for the pull request.

approvalRules.description: The approval rules applied to the pull request.

approvals.description: Information about users who have approved the pull request.

userArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user.

approvalState.description: The state of the approval, APPROVE or REVOKE. REVOKE states are not stored.

overridden.description: A Boolean value that indicates whether a pull request has had its rules set aside (TRUE) or whether all approval rules still apply (FALSE).

overrider.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user or identity that overrode the rules and their requirements for the pull request.

accountId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services account associated with the repository.

repositoryDescription.description: A comment or description about the repository.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the repository was last modified, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the repository was created, in timestamp format.

cloneUrlHttp.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over HTTPS.

cloneUrlSsh.description: The URL to use for cloning the repository over SSH.

Arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the repository.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service encryption key used to encrypt and decrypt the repository.

configurationId.description: The system-generated unique ID for the trigger.

triggers.description: The JSON block of configuration information for each trigger.

destinationArn.description: The ARN of the resource that is the target for a trigger (for example, the ARN of a topic in Amazon SNS).

customData.description: Any custom data associated with the trigger to be included in the information sent to the target of the trigger.

branches.description:
The branches to be included in the trigger configuration. If you specify an empty array, the trigger applies to all branches.

Although no content is required in the array, you must include the array itself.

events.description:
The repository events that cause the trigger to run actions in another service, such as sending a notification through Amazon SNS.

The valid value "all" cannot be used with any other values.

approvalRuleTemplateNames.description: The names of all approval rule templates associated with the repository.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the next results of the operation.
An enumeration token that returns the batch of the results.

revisionDag.description: An array of FileVersion objects that form a directed acyclic graph (DAG) of the changes to the file made by the commits that changed the file.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that can be used to return the next batch of results.

commitId.description: The full SHA ID of the specified commit.

parents.description: A list of parent commits for the specified commit. Each parent commit ID is the full commit ID.

message.description: The commit message associated with the specified commit.

author.description: Information about the author of the specified commit. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the author, and the email address for the author, as configured in Git.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

committer.description:
Information about the person who committed the specified commit, also known as the committer. Information includes the date in timestamp format with GMT offset, the name of the committer, and the email address for the committer, as configured in Git.

For more information about the difference between an author and a committer in Git, see Viewing the Commit History in Pro Git by Scott Chacon and Ben Straub.

name.description: The name of the user who made the specified commit.

email.description: The email address associated with the user who made the commit, if any.

date.description: The date when the specified commit was commited, in timestamp format with GMT offset.

additionalData.description: Any other data associated with the specified commit.

blobId.description: The blob ID of the object that represents the content of the file in this version.

path.description: The name and path of the file at which this blob is indexed which contains the data for this version of the file. This value will vary between file versions if a file is renamed or if its path changes.

revisionChildren.description: An array of commit IDs that contain more recent versions of this file. If there are no additional versions of the file, this array will be empty.

pullRequestIds.description: The system-generated IDs of the pull requests.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the next results of the operation.

repositoryNames.description: A list of repository names that are associated with the specified approval rule template.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the next results of the operation.

tags.description: A list of tag key and value pairs associated with the specified resource.

nextToken.description: An enumeration token that allows the operation to batch the next results of the operation.

commitId.description: The commit ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

treeId.description: The tree ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

commitId.description: The commit ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

treeId.description: The tree ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

commitId.description: The commit ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

treeId.description: The tree ID of the merge in the destination or target branch.

pullRequest.description: Information about the specified pull request, including the merge.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

title.description: The user-defined title of the pull request. This title is displayed in the list of pull requests to other repository users.

description.description: The user-defined description of the pull request. This description can be used to clarify what should be reviewed and other details of the request.

lastActivityDate.description: The day and time of the last user or system activity on the pull request, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date and time the pull request was originally created, in timestamp format.

pullRequestStatus.description: The status of the pull request. Pull request status can only change from OPEN to CLOSED.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who created the pull request.

pullRequestTargets.description: The targets of the pull request, including the source branch and destination branch for the pull request.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

revisionId.description: The system-generated revision ID for the pull request.

approvalRules.description: The approval rules applied to the pull request.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository where you posted a comment on the comparison between commits.

beforeCommitId.description: In the directionality you established, the full commit ID of the before commit.

afterCommitId.description: In the directionality you established, the full commit ID of the after commit.

beforeBlobId.description: In the directionality you established, the blob ID of the before blob.

afterBlobId.description: In the directionality you established, the blob ID of the after blob.

location.description: The location of the comment in the comparison between the two commits.

comment.description: The content of the comment you posted.

filePath.description: The name of the file being compared, including its extension and subdirectory, if any.

filePosition.description: The position of a change in a compared file, in line number format.

relativeFileVersion.description: In a comparison of commits or a pull request, whether the change is in the before or after of that comparison.

repositoryName.description: The name of the repository where you posted a comment on a pull request.

pullRequestId.description: The system-generated ID of the pull request.

beforeCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit in the source branch used to create the pull request, or in the case of an updated pull request, the full commit ID of the commit used to update the pull request.

afterCommitId.description: The full commit ID of the commit in the destination branch where the pull request is merged.

beforeBlobId.description: In the directionality of the pull request, the blob ID of the before blob.

afterBlobId.description: In the directionality of the pull request, the blob ID of the after blob.

location.description: The location of the change where you posted your comment.

comment.description: The content of the comment you posted.

filePath.description: The name of the file being compared, including its extension and subdirectory, if any.

filePosition.description: The position of a change in a compared file, in line number format.

relativeFileVersion.description: In a comparison of commits or a pull request, whether the change is in the before or after of that comparison.

comment.description: Information about the reply to a comment.

inReplyTo.description: The ID of the comment for which this comment is a reply, if any.

creationDate.description: The date and time the comment was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the comment was most recently modified, in timestamp format.

authorArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the person who posted the comment.

deleted.description: A Boolean value indicating whether the comment has been deleted.

clientRequestToken.description: A unique, client-generated idempotency token that, when provided in a request, ensures the request cannot be repeated with a changed parameter. If a request is received with the same parameters and a token is included, the request returns information about the initial request that used that token.

callerReactions.description: The emoji reactions to a comment, if any, submitted by the user whose credentials are associated with the call to the API.

reactionCounts.description: A string to integer map that represents the number of individual users who have responded to a comment with the specified reactions.

commitId.description: The full SHA ID of the commit that contains this file change.

blobId.description: The ID of the blob, which is its SHA-1 pointer.

treeId.description: The full SHA-1 pointer of the tree information for the commit that contains this file change.

configurationId.description: The system-generated unique ID for the create or update operation.

successfulExecutions.description: The list of triggers that were successfully tested. This list provides the names of the triggers that were successfully tested, separated by commas.

failedExecutions.description: The list of triggers that were not tested. This list provides the names of the triggers that could not be tested, separated by commas.

approvalRuleTemplate.description: Returns information about an approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateId.description: The system-generated ID of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateName.description: The name of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateDescription.description: The description of the approval rule template.

approvalRuleTemplateContent.description: The content of the approval rule template.

ruleContentSha256.description: The SHA-256 hash signature for the content of the approval rule template.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date the approval rule template was most recently changed, in timestamp format.

creationDate.description: The date the approval rule template was created, in timestamp format.

lastModifiedUser.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the user who made the most recent changes to the approval rule template.
